2605.27254.title: LUCoS: Latent Unsupervised Context Selection for Tabular Foundation Models
2605.27254.paper_summary: Selecting which instances to label is a key challenge in low-label tabular learning. For recent Tabular Foundation Models such as TabPFN, context selection directly determines predictive performance. Supervised oracle experiments show that carefully chosen labeled context sets can strongly outperform random selection under the same labeling budget. However, the cold-start setting, where instances must be selected before any labels are available, has received little attention in the TFM literature. This problem is fundamentally geometric. In vision and language, foundation models induce embedding spaces where simple geometric selection methods are effective. In contrast, tabular instance selection has so far been performed predominantly in the original tabular space, which lacks a natural metric; heterogeneous types, mixed scales, and nonlinear interactions make raw-space distances unreliable for context construction, and original-space selection falls below random on the majority of datasets as the budget grows. We propose LUCoS (Latent Unsupervised Context Selection), which replaces raw-feature geometry with the latent geometry induced by embeddings from an unsupervised Prior-Fitted Network (PFN) and selects representative medoids as context. Evaluated on 67 OpenML-CC18 datasets across six low-label budgets, LUCoS ranks first under mean AUC, ACC, and F1, with conclusions stable across metrics and dataset-level robustness checks. A gain decomposition reveals a simple mechanism: at the smallest budgets, the main benefit comes from enforcing coverage; as the budget increases, the decisive factor becomes the representation space in which coverage is measured. LUCoS mitigates failures of original feature space selection, showing that reliable unsupervised context selection depends less on selector sophistication than on defining representativeness in a meaningful representation geometry.
2605.27254.paper_summary_zh: 選擇標記哪些實例是低標籤表格學習中的一個關鍵挑戰。對於最近的表格基礎模型，如TabPFN，背景選擇直接決定預測性能。監督式預言實驗顯示，精心挑選的標記背景集在相同的標記預算下可以顯著超越隨機選擇。然而，冷啟動設置，即在任何標籤可用之前必須選擇實例，卻在TFM文獻中受到的關注很少。這個問題在根本上是幾何性的。在視覺和語言中，基礎模型引入了嵌入空間，在這裡簡單的幾何選擇方法是有效的。相比之下，表格實例選擇迄今為止主要是在原始表格空間中進行的，而該空間缺乏自然度量；異質類型、混合尺度和非線性互動使得原始空間距離對於背景構建不可靠，並且隨著預算的增加，原始空間選擇在大多數數據集上表現低於隨機。我們提出了LUCoS（潛在無監督背景選擇），它用來自無監督的Prior-Fitted Network（PFN）的嵌入所引起的潛在幾何替代原始特徵幾何，並選擇代表性的中位數作為背景。在67個OpenML-CC18數據集上進行評估，LUCoS在平均AUC、ACC和F1上排名第一，結論在各指標和數據集級別的穩健性檢查中穩定。增益分解揭示了一個簡單的機制：在最小的預算下，主要的好處來自於強制覆蓋；隨著預算的增加，決定性因素變成了測量覆蓋的表示空間。LUCoS減輕了原始特徵空間選擇的失敗，顯示出可靠的無監督背景選擇不太依賴於選擇器的複雜性，而是依賴於在有意義的表示幾何中定義代表性。
2605.27254.author: Oroel Ipas et.al.
2605.27254.authors: Oroel Ipas, Guillermo Gomez-Trenado, Rocío Romero-Zaliz, Isaac Triguero
